pros: Completes SQL persistence story; could support future dashboard queries.
cons: Adds DB credentials, DDL/migration and persistent state before a strong consumer exists.
risks: SQL write accidental; live confusion; MT5 proximity.
prerequisites: DDL approved, DB target policy, test DB, review, rollback plan.

option: Implement writer real only against local/test DB
pros: Tests append-only path without production DB.
cons: Still introduces DB connector, DDL and state management; may distract from pending platform phases.
risks: Credential handling; migration scope creep; false confidence.
prerequisites: Dedicated test DB, isolated schema, no production target, explicit review.

option: Keep preview-only and advance another phase
pros: Preserves safety evidence and avoids DB risk; frees effort for Telegram command bot, AI Analyst or dashboard/user review.

option: Defer SQL runtime to future work in memory
pros: Simplest safety posture; memory can describe design and gates without implementation burden.
risks: May leave a future integration gap.
prerequisites: Clear memory framing and roadmap entry.

option: Design AI Analyst / Telegram command bot / dashboard review before SQL real
cons: SQL runtime stays incomplete beyond preview.
prerequisites: Use artifacts/case packets instead of DB runtime writes.
